intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, encouraging language over clinical terminology when logging health data
facade.label: Casual check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: Adopt warm, motivational tone throughout the dashboard—'How are you feeling today?' instead of 'Input biometric data'
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into clinical precision and neutral framing—'Data entry', 'Measurement log', formal field labels

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards benefit from gentle, approachable shapes that feel less clinical and more wellness-oriented
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers softer visual language; avoid hard edges and clinical appearance in the design system
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer cleaner, more structured geometry; test crisper corners and minimal softening next

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer conversational, encouraging language over clinical terminology when logging health data
evidence.implication: Adopt warm, motivational tone throughout the dashboard—'How are you feeling today?' instead of 'Input biometric data'

facade.hypothesis: Health experiment tracking may benefit from chronological narrative (timeline showing progression over time) rather than spatial grid layout (all metrics visible at once).
facade.label: Timeline or grid
facade.acceptImplies: User values seeing how experiments unfolded over time; prefers story-like progression; might want annotations explaining 'why' changes happened.
facade.rejectImplies: User wants all current metrics visible simultaneously; prefers comparison view; cares more about status snapshot than journey.

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboard benefits from continuous, animated scrolling between experiment sections rather than instant page jumps
facade.label: Smooth page scroll
facade.acceptImplies: User wants motion that connects sections and creates continuity; prefers gliding over clicking-to-load
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers direct, low-friction navigation without motion overhead; values speed and instant state changes over visual flow

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards lean toward warm, organic visualization (gentle curves, earth tones, hand-drawn feel) rather than sharp clinical precision
facade.label: Soft data charts
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should feel human-centered and approachable—data rendered as living information, not sterile metrics. Warm color palette. Illustrated elements. Breathing space.
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer clean, minimal, precise aesthetic—clinical clarity over warmth. Sharp lines, neutral or cool palette, maximum information density. Data as objective fact, not experience.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users respond better to delightful, celebratory micro-interactions when logging data or hitting milestones, reinforcing positive behavior change over clinical feedback
facade.label: Cheerful progress animations
facade.acceptImplies: Invest in playful animations, emoji feedback, and reward-style confirmations to create emotional engagement with health tracking
facade.rejectImplies: Keep interactions minimal and matter-of-fact; users want efficiency and clarity, not distraction or gamification fatigue

facade.hypothesis: A persistent left sidebar for experiment navigation paired with card-based layouts for viewing metrics and insights creates a familiar, app-like feel that reduces cognitive load when switching between experiments.
facade.axisTargeted: LAYOUT—sidebar vs tabs navigation combined with content density and modularity
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values familiar app patterns, appreciates persistent navigation context, and wants clear visual separation between navigation and content areas.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer full-width layouts, tab-based navigation, or more immersive single-column designs that avoid sidebar real estate competition.

draft.title: Experiment Tracker Dashboard
draft.summary: A personal health dashboard for monitoring long-term experiments with visual progress tracking, experiment cards, and key metrics at a glance.
draft.html:
Health Experiments

Track your long-term wellness projects

Active

Days Avg

Current Experiments

Sleep Optimization

Tracking sleep quality with consistent bedtime

65% complete (39 days)
Active

Caffeine Reduction

Eliminating afternoon coffee intake

42% complete (25 days)
Active

Morning Hydration

Drinking 500ml water within 1 hour of waking

88% complete (53 days)
Paused
